title: achillea millefolium subsp. tanacetifolia
intro: Achillea millefolium subsp. tanacetifolia, also known as Tansy Fleabane, is a subspecies of the Yarrow plant, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small yellow flowers. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Tansy Fleabane include its ability to support the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote wound healing. Tansy Fleabane can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as added to salves and creams for topical application, making it a versatile and useful plant in natural medicine.
